the.d: the word used to point to a specific thing or person
the.e: The dog is chasing the cat.

to.d: a word that shows direction or movement
to.e: I need to go to the store.

that.d: a word that points to a specific thing or person
that.e: I found the book that I was looking for.

this.d: a word that points to something close by

they.d: a group of people or things

their.e: The children played with their toys.

time.d: a period of seconds, minutes, or hours
time.e: It's time to go to bed.

them.d: Them is a word used to talk about a group of people or things.
them.e: The kids are playing, and I want to join them.

than.d: Than is a word used to compare two things.
than.e: The apple is bigger than the orange.

two.d: Two is the number that comes after one.
two.e: I have two dogs as pets.

then.e: I do my chores, and then I get to play.

think.d: use your mind to have an idea or opinion
think.e: I think I want to go to the park today.

too.d: Also or in addition to something else
too.e: My friend is going to the park too.

take.e: I need to take my books to school today.

those.d: those things or people over there
those.e: I want to play with those toys on the shelf.

through.d: from one side to the other
through.e: I will walk through the park to get home.

three.d: The number that comes after two
three.e: I have three dogs to play with.

things.e: I like to play with many different things.

thing.e: My favorite thing to do is read books.

team.d: A group of people who work together to do something
team.e: My soccer team works hard to win the game.

thought.e: I had a good thought about what to do next.

times.e: I do my chores three times a day.

today.e: I play outside today when the weather is nice.

thanks.d: A word to show you are grateful
thanks.e: Thank you for helping me with my homework.

told.d: To say or communicate something to someone
told.e: The teacher told the class to be quiet.

tell.e: Can you tell me where the library is?

took.e: I took my dog for a walk earlier today.

top.e: The bird is sitting on top of the tree.

though.e: I'm tired though I had a good night's sleep.

thank.e: I said thank you when my teacher helped me.

try.d: to do your best to do or get something
try.e: I will try my best to win the race.

together.d: in a group or as a team
together.e: My friends and I played together at the park.

trying.e: She is trying her best to learn how to ride a bike.

true.e: It is true that the Earth orbits the sun.

talk.d: to say words out loud to someone
talk.e: I like to talk to my friends about our favorite movies.

taking.e: I'm taking my favorite book to read on the bus.

taken.d: when something or someone is no longer available
taken.e: The last cookie was already taken from the plate.

turn.d: to move in a different direction
turn.e: Please turn the page in your book.

third.d: The number that comes after second
third.e: I came in third place at the race.

type.d: A kind or category of something
type.e: My favorite type of flower is a sunflower.

talking.d: Using your voice to say words
talking.e: The kids were talking about their favorite animals.

total.d: The whole amount or number of something
total.e: The total cost of the trip was fifty dollars.

term.d: A word or short phrase that has a special meaning
term.e: The science term 'photosynthesis' is about how plants make food.

town.d: A small city with houses, stores, and other buildings
town.e: My town has a park, library, and lots of friendly people.

thinking.d: The process of using your mind to consider or decide something
thinking.e: I'm thinking hard about what I want to be when I grow up.

test.d: A way to check how much you have learned
test.e: I have a math test in class today.

trade.d: To give one thing and get another thing in return
trade.e: My brother traded his toy car for my toy truck.

tv.d: A device that shows pictures and stories
tv.e: I like to watch my favorite cartoon on TV.

themselves.d: To refer to a person or group doing something on their own.
themselves.e: The students cleaned up the classroom by themselves.

takes.e: I take my backpack to school every day.

training.e: The soccer team has training every week to get better.

terms.d: The words or phrases used to describe something.
terms.e: The teacher explained the terms we need to know for the test.

tried.d: To make an effort to do or accomplish something.
tried.e: I tried my best to finish my homework on time.

tax.d: Money that people pay to the government
tax.e: My parents have to pay taxes on their income every year.

table.d: A flat surface with legs to put things on.
table.e: We eat our meals at the kitchen table.

trust.d: Believing that someone or something is good and honest.
trust.e: I trust my teacher to help me learn.

turned.d: When something is moved to face a different direction
turned.e: The dog turned around when it heard a noise.

technology.d: The use of science and machines to do things.
technology.e: New technology makes computers work faster.

therefore.d: A word used to say the reason for something.
therefore.e: It was raining, therefore the picnic was cancelled.

towards.e: The bird flew towards the nest.

ten.d: The number that comes after nine
ten.e: I have ten fingers on my hands.

truth.d: Something that is real or honest
truth.e: My mom always tells me the truth.

thus.d: For that reason or in that way.
thus.e: I studied hard, thus I got a good grade on the test.

title.d: The name of a book, movie, or other work.
title.e: The title of my favorite book is 'The Hungry Caterpillar'.

text.d: The words written on a page
text.e: I can read the text in my favorite book

tomorrow.e: I'm going to the park with my friends tomorrow.

tonight.e: We're going to the movies tonight with our friends.

track.d: a path or line to follow
track.e: The runners followed the track around the field.

treatment.d: a way to help someone who is sick or hurt
treatment.e: The doctor gave the patient a special treatment to make them better.

throughout.e: The bird sang throughout the entire day.

train.d: A train is a vehicle that travels on tracks to carry people and things.
train.e: I love to ride on the fast train to the city.

tour.d: a trip to see places of interest
tour.e: We took a tour of the museum to see all the exhibits.

travel.d: To go from one place to another
travel.e: We love to travel to new places during the summer.

teams.d: A group of people who work together to do something.
teams.e: The sports teams practice hard to win their games.

traditional.d: Relating to the customs and beliefs of the past.
traditional.e: My family has a traditional way of celebrating holidays.

teacher.d: A person who helps students learn at school.
teacher.e: My teacher showed me how to do my math homework.

touch.d: When you use your hands to feel or interact with something.
touch.e: I gently touched the soft puppy's fur.

trip.d: A journey from one place to another place
trip.e: My family took a trip to the beach last summer.

telling.d: To say or explain something to someone.
telling.e: My friend is telling me a funny story.

theory.d: An idea that explains how something works.
theory.e: My teacher has a theory about how birds fly.

types.e: There are many types of animals in the zoo.

television.d: An electronic device used to watch shows and movies
television.e: We like to watch our favorite TV shows on the big screen in the living room.

till.d: Until a certain time or point
till.e: I have to finish my homework till bedtime tonight.

trial.d: A test to see how well something works
trial.e: We had a trial run of the new game before the big tournament.

target.d: Something you try to hit or reach
target.e: The archer aimed at the target.

tree.d: A tall plant with a trunk and branches
tree.e: The squirrel climbed up the tall tree.

traffic.d: The cars, trucks, and buses on the road
traffic.e: There was a lot of traffic on the highway this morning.

twice.e: I read the book twice because I liked it so much.

throw.d: To send something through the air with your hand
throw.e: The baseball player threw the ball to the catcher.

tom.e: Tom is playing soccer with his friends in the park.

thomas.e: My friend Thomas likes to play soccer.

trouble.e: I got in trouble for staying up too late.

truly.e: The puppy is truly the cutest dog I've ever seen.

teachers.d: People who help students learn at school
teachers.e: My favorite teacher makes learning fun and interesting.

tells.d: To say or share information with someone
tells.e: The news reporter tells us important information every day.

thousands.d: A very large number, more than hundreds
thousands.e: There were thousands of people at the big concert.

transport.d: To move people or things from one place to another
transport.e: The school bus transports students to and from school.

texas.d: A very large state in the southern part of the United States.
texas.e: Texas is known for its wide open spaces and cowboy culture.

taste.d: the feeling in your mouth when you eat or drink something
taste.e: The cookie had a sweet taste.

thinks.d: to have ideas or beliefs in your mind
thinks.e: The student thinks about the answer carefully.

thoughts.d: ideas or feelings in your mind
thoughts.e: Her thoughts were focused on the test.

turns.e: The car turned the corner quickly.

tea.d: A type of drink that is made from leaves of a special plant
tea.e: I drink a cup of tea every morning with my breakfast.

technical.d: Involving skills or knowledge, like how to use machines or computers
technical.e: The new video game has a lot of technical features that are fun to learn.

tough.d: Something that is very hard to break or do
tough.e: My mom says I need to be tough and keep trying when things are difficult.

treat.d: A special food or activity that is fun and makes you happy
treat.e: My parents let me have a special treat after I finish my homework.

trees.d: Tall plants with a trunk and branches that have leaves
trees.e: We have a big tree in our backyard that gives us shade on hot days.

twitter.d: A social media app where people can share short messages
twitter.e: My friend uses Twitter to share funny jokes and updates about her day.

terrible.e: The thunderstorm was a terrible storm with lots of lightning.

territory.d: A place that belongs to someone or something
territory.e: The lion protects its territory from other animals.

tests.d: Challenges to see what you have learned
tests.e: The students took a spelling test in class.

transfer.d: To move something from one place to another
transfer.e: The teacher helped the student transfer to a new school.

treated.d: When someone is given something special
treated.e: The dog was treated to a special treat for being a good boy.

troops.d: Groups of soldiers who work together
troops.e: The troops marched into the battle.

task.d: A piece of work that needs to be done
task.e: My chore is to clean my room, which is my task.

teaching.d: The job of helping others learn and understand things
teaching.e: My teacher is very good at teaching the class.

turning.e: I heard the car turning the corner.

thousand.d: A very large number, equal to one followed by three zeros.
thousand.e: There were thousands of people at the concert.

taught.d: When someone shows or tells you how to do something.
taught.e: The teacher taught the class how to spell new words.

temperature.d: How hot or cold something is.
temperature.e: The weather report said the temperature will be 75 degrees today.

tired.d: When you feel sleepy or don't have any energy.
tired.e: After a long day at school, the student was feeling tired.

twenty.d: The number that comes after nineteen
twenty.e: I have twenty books on my shelf.

teach.d: to show someone how to do something
teach.e: My teacher teaches us how to read.

testing.d: When you try something to see how well it works.
testing.e: The teacher gave us a testing quiz to see what we learned.

theme.d: The main idea or topic of something.
theme.e: The theme of the story was how to be a good friend.

threat.d: Something that could cause trouble or harm.
threat.e: The big dog was a threat to the small cat.

taxes.d: Money that people pay to the government
taxes.e: We have to pay our taxes every year.

taylor.d: A person who makes or repairs clothes
taylor.e: The taylor altered my dress to fit better.

tuesday.d: The day of the week that comes after Monday
tuesday.e: My class has art on Tuesdays.

thursday.d: Thursday is the 4th day of the week, after Wednesday and before Friday.
thursday.e: I have piano lessons every Thursday afternoon.

talked.d: To say words out loud to someone.
talked.e: The friends talked about their favorite books.

tank.d: A large container that holds liquid or gas.
tank.e: The car needs more gas in its fuel tank.

tech.d: The use of science and machines in everyday life.
tech.e: The new tech gadget has many cool features.

tools.d: Things used to do a specific job or task.
tools.e: The carpenter uses many different tools to build furniture.

toronto.e: The family went on a trip to visit Toronto.

turkey.d: A large bird that is often eaten for Thanksgiving.
turkey.e: The family cooked a big turkey for their Thanksgiving dinner.

typical.d: Something that is normal or usual.
typical.e: The weather today is typical for this time of year.

ticket.d: A small piece of paper that lets you do something.
ticket.e: The ticket lets me ride the bus to school.

tradition.d: Something your family or group has done for a long time.
tradition.e: Every year we have the tradition of decorating the Christmas tree.

talent.d: A special skill or ability that someone has.
talent.e: My friend has a real talent for drawing.

tend.d: To usually do or be a certain way.
tend.e: Puppies tend to be very playful and energetic.

trained.d: To teach someone how to do something properly.
trained.e: The dog was trained to sit and stay on command.

typically.d: In the usual or normal way.
typically.e: My sister typically does her homework in the evening.

teeth.d: Hard, white parts in your mouth used for biting and chewing
teeth.e: I brush my teeth every morning and night.

tool.d: An object used to do a job or work
tool.e: A hammer is a useful tool for building things.

tower.d: A tall, narrow building, often part of a larger structure
tower.e: The castle has a tall tower you can climb.

truck.d: A large vehicle used to carry things.
truck.e: The truck delivered the new furniture to our house.

talks.d: to say words out loud to someone
talks.e: The teacher talks to the class during the lesson.

tall.d: being high or long from the ground to the top
tall.e: My little brother is very tall for his age.

tight.d: not loose or able to move freely
tight.e: The shoes were too tight and hurt my feet.

tiny.e: The baby bird was so tiny it could fit in my hand.

tournament.d: a competition where people play a game or sport to win
tournament.e: The school is hosting a chess tournament this weekend.

transportation.d: a way to move from one place to another
transportation.e: We use a car for transportation to get to school.

techniques.d: Special ways of doing something skillfully
techniques.e: The artist used special painting techniques.

thats.d: Used to point to or refer to something
thats.e: That is my favorite book on the shelf.

theatre.d: A special place where people go to watch shows or movies.
theatre.e: We went to the theatre to see a play.

tickets.d: Small pieces of paper that let you go into a show or event.
tickets.e: I bought tickets to the concert.

trading.e: The students were trading stickers during recess.

tip.d: The top or end of something
tip.e: She put a tip on the pencil.

temporary.d: Something that doesn't last a long time
temporary.e: The rain shower was only temporary.

tie.d: A piece of cloth you wear around your neck
tie.e: My dad wore a nice blue tie to work.

temple.d: A building for people to worship and pray in
temple.e: The ancient temple was an important place of worship.

treaty.e: The countries signed a treaty to work together.

trend.d: A general direction that something is moving in
trend.e: The new fashion trend is wearing bright colors.

tries.d: To make an effort to do something
tries.e: She tries her best to be a good friend to everyone.

threw.d: To send something through the air
threw.e: The player threw the ball to his teammate.

tears.d: The drops of water that come from your eyes, often when you are sad.
tears.e: The little girl cried tears of joy when she got the new toy.

tim.e: Tim played catch with his dog in the backyard.

trick.d: A clever or surprising action that is done to fool or entertain people.
trick.e: The magician performed a trick to make the rabbit disappear.
